batch_objective: US-002..US-005 as one interconnected feature set: (002) single hex-first JSON change system — address-only entries, encoding-aware string/byte patches, intra-file collision checks, change summary with informative MAC/A2L linkage; retires the cfdx/.cdfx parameter flow and consolidates the Patch Editor UI. (003) declarative check files (expected values vs loaded S19), automatable per project. (004) Markdown project report under .s19tool/workarea/<project>/reports/ — modified files, before->after values, executed checks, +/-64-byte adjustable hexdumps — also viewable in the TUI. (005) N S19 variants per project sharing one A2L + one MAC, batch or per-variant application, all reflected in the report. Plus CI-1: two-tier CI gate (PRs -m 'not slow', full suite on main push) in the first PR. Engine/parsers consumed read-only.
branch: claude/batch-07 (worktree; branched off main post-PR-#11; one local commit per increment, PR at batch close)
batch_layout: Each batch archived under .dev-flow/<YYYY-MM-DD>-batch-NN/. Living docs (REQUIREMENTS.md, tests/, docs/diagrams/) kept current. On batch advance, the closing batch's state.json is snapshotted into its folder as state-snapshot-at-close.json before the shared state.json is trimmed to the new batch.

decisions_log.notes: Batch-06 (US-001 MAC proportional+floor) fully closed: 6 phases, PR #10 merged (9fb25b2), post-mortem actions applied — A-1 env-measurement rule into req-template-en.md, A-2 R-TUI-039 superseded, A-3 CI main-PR trigger via PR #11 (648cf82; exposed+fixed 2 latent CI breakages: bare-pytest tests-import -> pythonpath=['.'], missing Pillow; first full-green 3.11 run 792 passed/0 failed in 33:18), A-5 discarded (typo; A-3 resolves it), A-8 synced to canonical G: vault. Full batch-06 history: .dev-flow/2026-06-09-batch-06/state-snapshot-at-close.json. Carried into batch-07: A-4 (measured disposition tables — now a template-level rule in this batch's 01-requirements header), A-6 (Phase-4 run-ownership), A-9 (knee-point test), CI-cost optimization (decided: two-tier, lands as CI-1).

Operator requested planning of the remaining original stories (US-002..US-005, refined 2026-06-09) + open action items, then /dev-flow-init. Increment plan approved at creation (§6.5): E1 JSON v2 schema/validator -> E2 apply engine + linkage summary -> E3 UI consolidation + cfdx retirement (measured disposition table REQUIRED) -> E4 check engine -> E5 multi-S19 workspace/variant model -> E6 batch/per-variant execution -> E7 report generator -> E8 report UI + E2E; CI-1 rides the first PR. Three design decisions locked at planning gate (2026-06-10): (1) ADDRESS-ONLY entries — hex-first pure, MAC/A2L linkage informative only; changelist.py + CDFX XML I/O + parameter UI retire entirely; (2) two-tier CI — PRs -m 'not slow', full suite on main push; (3) Markdown report under .s19tool/workarea/<project>/reports/ + TUI view. Scaffolded .dev-flow/2026-06-10-batch-07/ per project convention; 01-requirements.md seeded with §1-2, all 4 stories + locked decisions in §2.6, anchor candidates §6.4 (from the 2026-06-09 codebase map — re-grep at Phase-1 draft), increment plan §6.5, and the NEW measured-test-disposition rule + environmental-measurement rule in the normative header. Risk register seeded §6.3 (cfdx test surface ~192 tests; LoadedFile multi-variant redesign; REQUIREMENTS.md §8-9 supersession; report size caps).

Phase 1 wave 1 executed: 2 architects in parallel (A: US-002/003 -> HLR-001..004 + 24 LLRs, v2 schema + changes/ package design + v1 hard-break recommendation; B: US-004/005 -> HLR-005..008 + 25 LLRs, descriptor-set variant model avoiding the 166-occurrence LoadedFile blast radius [measured: rg -c, 2026-06-10], project.json manifest, report layout) + qa-reviewer producing the MEASURED test-disposition table (header rule): 355 in scope = 229 RETIRE / 50 REWRITE / 69 SURVIVES / 7 DEPENDS-ON-DESIGN, collector-reconciled (344 collected over 23 files + 11 strays); supersedes the ~192 estimate. Orchestrator integrated all into 01-requirements.md: 8 HLR / 49 LLR / TC-001..050 index / C-1..C-9 design decisions incl. C-6 cross-architect interface reconciliation (ChangeSummary/CheckRunResult canonical field sets — producers 002.5/004.3, consumers 006.5/007.8; Phase-2 must verify identity); disposition table embedded as §6.6 with D1/D2 resolved by LLR-001.5 (pending operator confirmation), D3 deferred to Phase-3 measurement. Normative self-check: 0 'should' outside header. 6 gate-confirmable items in §6.3 incl. ONE open scope gap: save-back of the patched image to disk (report's 'modified files' implies persistence; nothing locked). Validation strategy §5 assembled by orchestrator from architects' embedded verifications (A-6 economy); Phase-2 qa will adversarially review it. Awaiting gate + operator answers.

All 6 gate-confirmable items DECIDED by operator (2026-06-10): (1) value_mode text/codes CONFIRMED; (2) collision ERROR with nuance — blocking error applies to collisions between declared patch operations; declaration faults additionally reported in the project report's error section AND rendered persistently visible in the app (NEW LLR-002.8 + LLR-007.4 declaration-error subsection); (3) hex-string addresses CONFIRMED; (4) v1 HARD BREAK confirmed — no shim, no migrator; v1 files inert on disk as unsupported history; operator rationale recorded (cfdx retired to avoid two formats; linkage informative — its purpose is the before->after report sense-check, e.g. catching a multiplier-vs-quantity write; observed in report, never blocked by rules) and embedded in HLR-007 rationale; (5) project.json manifest YES; (6) save-back YES to workarea with operator-prompted filename (NEW HLR-002 stmt 5 + LLR-002.7, saved_path in summary, CLI patch-hex serialization path assumed-verify-Phase-3). Doc now 8 HLR / 51 LLR. Awaiting final Phase-1 gate decision (approve -> Phase 2).

Phase 1 approved at gate ('aprobar'). 8 HLR / 51 LLR / TC-001..050 / disposition table embedded / all 6 confirmables decided. Operator ALSO registered a NEW user story for the NEXT batch (batch-08), saved to persistent memory (project_us006_hex_compare_mode.md): US-006 — comparison mode for 2+ hex/s19 images (in-project or external) sharing the same A2L/MAC, with an explicit note of which artifacts each image uses (both/one/none), generating a diff report; N-way if feasible. Natural surface: the A-B Diff rail placeholder (screens_directionb.py ~:1334). NOT in batch-07 scope. Advancing to Phase 2 cross-review.

Phase 2. architect (re-review: completeness/contradictions/derivation/C-6 interface identity) + qa-reviewer (testability/method viability/threshold soundness/disposition-table spot-audit) + security-reviewer (REAL surface this batch: JSON file ingestion with encodings, patched-image writes, project.json manifest, headless entry points) dispatched in parallel. Output: 02-review.md.

Phase 2 returned 4 unique blockers / 17 majors / 13 minors / security OK-with-mitigations. CLEAN: shall-should, derivation, anchor hygiene (100+ re-verified, 'best of any batch'), env-measurement rule, disposition table (totals re-add, 10/10 sampled rows correct, collector 344 re-confirmed). BLOCKERS: B-1 C-6 canonical schema stale vs late LLRs 002.7/002.8 (saved_path/issues consumed by report but absent from producer contract — found independently by architect AND qa); B-2 CheckRunResult has no issues carrier (check declaration faults drop from the report chain); B-3 LLR-003.3 retirement grep uses BRE where | is literal — guaranteed false-pass; B-4 double-apply determinism test cannot pass (2nd apply sees patched mem_map; timestamps differ). KEY MAJORS: F-A-05 save-back reuse assumption FALSE (CLI serializes S19File object, engine is mem_map-pure; hexfile.py has NO writer — HEX save-back unimplementable via existing paths); F-A-07 E3 exceeds 5-file cap unacknowledged; F-A-08 check-collision semantics inherited unexamined; F-Q-08 ~60 SURVIVES rows import cdfx.* and die at collection post-E3 (definition fix); F-S-02 VERIFIED LIVE non-text codecs pass codecs.lookup then raise LookupError uncaught; F-S-06 VERIFIED LIVE Textual Markdown open_links=True default opens system browser. Per dev-flow rule blockers force Phase-1 iteration. 3 operator decisions needed: HEX save-back scope, E3/E5 increment-budget exception vs split, check-collision severity. Awaiting gate.

Operator gate decisions: D-1 save-back S19-only (HEX emitter = batch-08 candidate, .hex prompt declines with saved_path=None, NEW emit_s19_from_mem_map in changes/io.py — false CLI-reuse claim withdrawn); D-2 SPLIT increments E3->E3a/E3b + E5->E5a/E5b (E3b carries explicit ~20-25-file budget exception, predominantly deletions/test re-pointing); D-3 intra-check collisions ERROR uniform (WARNING alternative recorded REJECTED). architect dispatched with the full 02-review.md fix register; applied ALL 34 findings + 3 decisions to 01-requirements.md (394->444 lines) with §6.7 reconciliation audit (45 rows, body-first per template rule). Orchestrator independently verified load-bearing closures: B-1/B-2 saved_path+issues through C-6/002.5/004.3/006.5/007.4; B-3 grep -rE + self-tested probe; B-4 injectable now_fn clock; F-S-02 text-encoding allowlist; F-S-06 open_links=False; TC-051/052 assigned without renumbering (roll-up TC-053); F-Q-04 strict-wire/permissive-TUI grammar split; F-Q-08 SURVIVES redefined (imports re-pointed = E3b task). 0 'should' in statements. Re-confirmation appended to 02-review.md: 4/4 blockers + 17/17 majors + 13/13 minors CLOSED in one iteration. Awaiting Phase-2 gate.

Phase-2 re-confirmation gate approved ('apruebo. continúa'). Advancing to Phase 3 — increment plan E1, E2, E3a, E3b, E4, E5a, E5b, E6, E7, E8 (10 increments). Local commit per increment in the worktree (rollback points); PR + CI-1 at batch close per project convention.
